title: hieracium crassiceps
intro: Hieracium crassiceps, also known as Rough Hawkweed, is a flowering plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) and native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of Hieracium crassiceps lie in its leaves and roots, which contain sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. It has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, offering health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating skin conditions like eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, Hieracium crassiceps is often used as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be consumed as a raw supplement or added to topical creams and ointments for skin care applications.
